purpose: First student computation with MC scaffolding; same family as worked example.
visual: Context Visualization: 8 bags with 2 items each. Equation Builder: `8 × 2 = [___]`. Product slot has MC options: 10, 14, 16, 18.
guide: "Your turn. 8 times 2. That means 8 groups of 2. Skip-count by 2s — eight times. Pick the product of 8 times 2."
student_action: Equation Builder Method A (MC on product slot)
options:
- 10 (added 8 + 2)
	- 14 (skip-count error — 7 counts, i.e. worked example answer)
	- 16 ✓
	- 18 (skip-count error — 9 counts)
on_correct: "8 times 2 equals 16. Notice that it's even — fits the 2s pattern. You built the equation."
remediation_note: Have student skip-count by 2s explicitly — count 8 twos: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16.
design_note: Example-problem pair — student tries similar problem immediately after worked example. Same factor family (2s), same context (bags), MC method (lower cognitive demand for first equation attempt). Value changed from 4×2 to 8×2 to avoid repeating W.1's exact problem; 14 distractor catches students pulling the worked example answer (7×2=14) instead of computing fresh. First student computation in the lesson — appropriately placed after worked example.
